title: What to know about Passiflora incarnata before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Passiflora incarnata are treating anxiety through its flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anxiolytic properties. It also alleviates insomnia due to its sedative effects, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids like passiflorine and chrysin. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been used to manage epilepsy thanks to its anticonvulsant properties, which are associated with the flavonoids and alkaloids, as well as restlessness and hyperactivity in children, resulting from its sedative effects. Furthermore, it has been employed to reduce hypertension due to its vasodilatory properties, which are linked to the flavonoids and alkaloids. Its flavonoids and passiflorine also provide relief from menstrual cramps, hysterical episodes, and nervousness, while its flavonoids and alkaloids help to alleviate digestive issues.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Passiflora incarnata are multifaceted, as it exhibits antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, which is attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins. Its antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, both of which are associated with the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata has been found to possess analgesic properties, providing relief from pain, which is partly due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. Additionally, it has sedative and antispasmodic effects, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate muscle spasms, respectively, due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils. Its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, also contribute to its antihypertensive, anticonvulsant, and anxiolytic effects, providing cardiovascular protection and alleviating anxiety and seizure disorders.
identification: Passiflora incarnata is a perennial, evergreen, and climbing vine native to North America, with a distribution that spans eastern and south-central regions, including parts of Canada and the United States, and extending into northern Mexico. It features palmate leaves with 5-7 lobes, 3-5 inches in diameter, and 1-2 inches long, with a central leaflet and two smaller lateral leaflets. The leaves are dark green, alternate, and have a leathery texture. The vine produces yellowish-green flowers, 1-2 inches in diameter, in the summer months, followed by egg-shaped, green, and 3-4 inches long fruits that ripen to purple. Passiflora incarnata typically thrives in moist, well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions.
cultivation: Passiflora incarnata is a vigorous, perennial vine native to the southeastern United States, exhibiting a twining growth habit that can reach up to 8 meters in length. To thrive, it requires full sun to partial shade and a warm climate with average temperatures above 15°C, well-draining soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, and consistent moisture, although it can tolerate some drought. When cultivating Passiflora incarnata, it's essential to provide a trellis or other support to accommodate its rapid growth, prune it regularly to maintain a manageable size, and avoid over-fertilizing, as this can lead to weak and spindly growth. To promote healthy growth, it's recommended to repot the plant every 2-3 years in the spring, using a slightly larger pot and fresh potting mix, and to mulch around the base to retain moisture and suppress weeds. By following these guidelines, Passiflora incarnata can be successfully grown in a variety of settings, from containers to large gardens.
history: Passiflora incarnata is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily in the indigenous cultures of North America. The plant, also known as maypop or passionflower, has been employed to treat various ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and seizures. Native American tribes used it to make a tea for relief from convulsions and to calm nervous conditions. Early European settlers adopted its use, and it was later incorporated into the United States' pharmacopeia in 1831, where it was used to treat conditions like delirium tremens and hiccups. In 1926, the plant was recognized as a sedative and antispasmodic by the US Pharmacopeia, with its dried herb being used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a type of constituent found in Passiflora incarnata, characterized by antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of glycoside constituent in Passiflora incarnata that exhibit hemolytic, foaming, and surfactant properties

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Alkaloids in Passiflora incarnata include harman, harmine, and harmaline, which are indole alkaloids that exhibit sedative and hypnotic effects.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides are a type of compound found in Passiflora incarnata, characterized by a sugar molecule bonded to a non-carbohydrate moiety, and have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are bioactive compounds with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Sterols are a type of constituent in Passiflora incarnata, known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids found in Passiflora incarnata are a class of bioactive compounds known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: A type of phytochemical found in Passiflora incarnata, with antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids are a class of compounds found in Passiflora incarnata with sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Fatty Acids
constituents.description: Fatty Acids in Passiflora incarnata are composed of palmitic, linoleic, and oleic acids, which are saturated and unsaturated fatty acids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: Lignans are a type of polyphenolic compound found in Passiflora incarnata, exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are a type of polyphenol with astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Fatty acids
constituents.description: Fatty acids in Passiflora incarnata have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides are a type of bioactive compound found in Passiflora incarnata, characterized by the presence of a phenolic aglycone and a glycosidic moiety.

constituents.name: Carotenoids
constituents.description: Carotenoids are yellow, orange, and red pigments with antioxidant properties, including beta-carotene and lutein.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are a type of plant-derived steroid, with properties including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: Luteolin is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Steroids
constituents.description: Steroids found in Passiflora incarnata, specifically passifloric acid and passiflorin, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Dizziness occurs due to decreased blood pressure following ingestion of Passiflora incarnata.
Nausea occurs as a result of Passiflora incarnata consumption.
Ingestion of Passiflora incarnata may cause gastrointestinal upset, leading to vomiting.
Passiflora incarnata can cause central nervous system depression leading to physical tremors.
Passiflora incarnata causes a decrease in muscle strength.
Passiflora incarnata increases respiratory depression by depressing respiration due to its barbiturate-like activity.
Passiflora incarnata causes a decrease in central nervous system activity leading to sedation.
Central nervous system depression, including altered mental status, drowsiness, and coma, has been reported.
Passiflora incarnata decreases heart rate, potentially causing bradycardia.
Passiflora incarnata causes a significant decrease in blood pressure due to its vasodilatory and cardiac-inhibitory effects.

intro_description: Passiflora incarnata, commonly known as maypop, is a herb used for medicinal purposes. Its main medicinal uses are in treating anxiety disorders, insomnia, and restlessness. The herb also provides health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality. Passiflora incarnata contains therapeutic properties including sedative, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive compounds present in the herb include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, often used to prepare a tea infusion.

uses_list.description: Passiflora incarnata is used for anxiety due to its ability to inhibit the activity of the enzyme monoamine oxidase (MAO), which breaks down neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, thereby increasing their availability in the brain and promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Passiflora incarnata is used for insomnia due to its sedative properties, which help to reduce sleep latency and improve sleep quality by acting on the central nervous system, particularly the gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) receptors, to induce relaxation and promote sleep.

Passiflora incarnata is used for depression due to its ability to inhibit the reuptake of serotonin, a neurotransmitter involved in mood regulation, thereby increasing its availability in the brain and promoting a sense of well-being and reducing depressive symptoms.

Passiflora incarnata is used for epilepsy due to its anticonvulsant properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures by acting on the central nervous system, particularly the GABA receptors, to regulate neuronal activity and prevent the spread of seizure activity.

Passiflora incarnata is used for hypertension due to its ability to reduce blood pressure by acting on the peripheral blood vessels, particularly the alpha-adrenergic receptors, to cause vasodilation and reduce peripheral resistance.

Passiflora incarnata is used for migraine due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraine headaches by acting on the trigeminal nerve and reducing the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters.

Passiflora incarnata is used for chronic pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the intensity and duration of chronic pain by acting on the central and peripheral nervous systems to reduce pain perception and promote relaxation.

Passiflora incarnata is used for restlessness due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce restlessness and promote relaxation by acting on the central nervous system, particularly the GABA receptors, to induce a calming effect.

Passiflora incarnata is used for stress due to its adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce stress and promote relaxation by acting on the central nervous system to regulate the body's response to stress and promote balance and well-being.

Passiflora incarnata is used for hyperactivity due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce hyperactivity and promote relaxation by acting on the central nervous system, particularly the GABA receptors, to induce a calming effect and reduce excessive activity.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat anxiety, a condition characterized by excessive worry and fear. This herbal remedy has also been shown to be effective in managing insomnia, a sleep disorder marked by difficulty falling or staying asleep. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been used to alleviate symptoms of depression, a mood disorder characterized by persistent feelings of sadness and hopelessness. Research has also demonstrated its potential in treating epilepsy, a neurological disorder involving seizures, as well as hypertension, high blood pressure that can lead to heart disease. Furthermore, it has been found to be beneficial in reducing symptoms of nausea, restlessness, chronic pain, migraines, pain, restless leg syndrome, seizures, headaches, sleep disorder, muscle spasm, irritability, stress, hyperactivity, and menstrual cramps.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat various health conditions, primarily anxiety and insomnia, which are alleviated by its sedative and anxiolytic properties. The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids also help manage hypertension by reducing blood pressure and heart rate, and it has been observed to have a positive effect on depression in patients with bipolar disorder. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat epilepsy, particularly absence seizures, and chronic pain, as well as migraines, which are often associated with headaches. It also has applications in the treatment of rheumatism, digestive issues, menopausal symptoms, nausea, gastroesophageal reflux disease, and stress, often caused by restlessness and seizures. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory properties make it an effective treatment for inflammation, headaches, menopause, and sleep disorders.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and stress by promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, while its sedative properties also help alleviate epilepsy and seizures. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat depression, hypertension, and nervousness by regulating emotions and blood pressure. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat migraines, muscle spasms, gastroesophageal reflux, and menopause symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Its antispasmodic properties also help alleviate chronic pain, rheumatism, and hyperactivity in children, whereas its anxiolytic and sedative effects help reduce panic attacks and sleep disorders. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also help alleviate inflammation, making it a useful treatment for various health conditions, including seizures, which is a symptom of epilepsy.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and depression by reducing symptoms of anxiety disorders and promoting relaxation. It is also effective in managing hypertension, epilepsy, and migraine through its antispasmodic properties and flavonoid content, which helps to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat hyperactivity, restlessness, panic disorder, and chronic pain by reducing anxiety and stress levels in the body. Its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties also make it useful in treating rheumatism, restless leg syndrome, inflammation, nausea, and asthma, while also alleviating menopause symptoms, stress, menstrual cramps, seizures, and fever. Furthermore, it has been found to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures and provide relief from menstrual cramps in women.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Passiflora incarnata is used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and depression, by modulating the neurotransmitters serotonin and gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Additionally, it has been found to be effective in managing epilepsy by reducing seizure activity, and hypertension through its vasodilatory effects. Passiflora incarnata also helps to calm restlessness, nausea, and inflammation, while also providing relief from stress, migraines, pain, and panic disorder. Furthermore, it is used to treat sleep disorders, migraines, hyperactivity, digestive issues, restless leg syndrome, headaches, menstrual cramps, and gastrointestinal issues. By regulating these conditions, Passiflora incarnata has become a popular homeopathic remedy for promoting relaxation and overall well-being.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids in this plant, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, thereby reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

The sedative properties of this plant, primarily due to the flavonoids and alkaloids, help to induce sleep and improve sleep quality by reducing the time taken to fall asleep and increasing the duration of sleep.
The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant help to reduce stress symptoms by mitigating the negative effects of stress on the body and promoting a sense of calm and well-being.

This plant has been traditionally used to aid in weight loss due to its ability to reduce appetite and improve metabolism, although the exact mechanisms are not fully understood and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
The sedative properties of this plant make it a potential treatment for insomnia, as it can help to induce sleep and improve sleep quality in individuals with difficulty sleeping.
The phytoestrogens present in this plant may help to alleviate menopause symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings, by reducing the severity and frequency of these symptoms.
The flavonoids and alkaloids in this plant have been shown to have a positive effect on mood and may help to relieve symptoms of depression, such as improved mood and reduced anxiety.
The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant, primarily due to the flavonoids and alkaloids, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions.
The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant may help to improve digestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
The antioxidant properties of this plant help to protect against oxidative stress and promote immune function, thereby boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of illness and disease.

benefits_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces anxiety by containing flavonoids and alkaloids, which modulate the activity of neurotransmitters in the brain. Improves sleep quality is also a benefit of the plant, attributed to its sedative effects, which are enhanced by the presence of myristicin and harmine, compounds that interact with neurotransmitter receptors. Relieves stress symptoms through the inhibition of stress-related hormones and the promotion of relaxation, a function facilitated by the anxiolytic properties of the plant's extracts. Passiflora incarnata supports weight loss by suppressing appetite and reducing fat accumulation due to its ability to decrease the expression of genes involved in lipid metabolism, an effect linked to its flavonoid content. Treats insomnia and eases menopause symptoms through its sedative and antispasmodic effects, respectively, which are also responsible for relieving depression and reducing inflammation.

The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce blood pressure, contributing to cardiovascular health. Additionally, its flavonoids enhance vasodilation, promoting healthy blood vessel expansion, and increase circulation, allowing for efficient delivery of oxygen and nutrients. Passiflora incarnata supports heart health by lowering cholesterol levels and regulating blood vessels, which is attributed to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Its ability to regulate blood vessels and improve cardiac function also increases oxygen delivery, reducing inflammation and promoting overall cardiovascular well-being.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Passiflora incarnata reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are responsible for its soothing effects on the digestive system. It soothes irritation and relaxes muscles due to its antispasmodic and muscle relaxant properties, providing relief from cramping and spasms. The plant relieves spasms and calms diarrhea by exerting its antimicrobial and anti-diarrheal properties, which prevent the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and reduce fluid loss. By exerting its laxative and antiulcer properties, the plant alleviates constipation, cures dyspepsia, treats hyperacidity, and heals ulcers, making it an effective treatment for various gastrointestinal disorders.

Stabilizes insulin production in individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its antioxidant properties, which counteract oxidative stress-induced pancreatic damage. Reduces cortisol levels by exhibiting anti-anxiety and sedative effects, attributed to its gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) receptor activity. Balances thyroid function by regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Passiflora incarnata decreases adrenaline levels by interacting with adrenergic receptors and exhibits other medicinal properties such as anxiolytic and sedative effects, which enhance ovarian function and support pancreatic function, while relaxing the pituitary gland and improving metabolism, and finally stimulates thyroid function through its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Passiflora incarnata reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties. It prevents cell damage by scavenging free radicals through its antioxidant activity, thus protecting the skin from oxidative stress. The plant increases skin elasticity by stimulating the production of collagen and elastin, which are essential for maintaining skin firmness and resilience. Passiflora incarnata soothes skin irritations by exerting its antimicrobial and anti-allergic effects, making it an effective remedy for skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. The medicinal properties of Passiflora incarnata, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, improve wound healing, fight skin infections, enhance collagen production, protect against sun damage, calm skin sensitivities, and promote healthy regeneration, ultimately supporting the health and integrity of the integumentary system.

By stimulating white blood cell production and activating lymph nodes, Passiflora incarnata promotes tissue repair, improves circulation rapidly, eases swelling naturally, and stimulates lymphatic drainage, ultimately contributing to a healthy and balanced lymphatic system.

It also reduces muscle spasms by exhibiting a sedative effect on the nervous system, inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that cause muscle contractions. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata eases muscle tension through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and muscle strain. Furthermore, Passiflora incarnata soothes muscle cramps, prevents muscle damage, heals tissue injuries, relaxes muscle fibers, and improves flexibility by modulating the activity of calcium channels and promoting the release of relaxing neurotransmitters, ultimately leading to a reduction in muscle spasms and an improvement in musculoskeletal function.

Additionally, Passiflora incarnata soothes stress by exerting a sedative effect on the central nervous system, and relaxing muscles through its muscle relaxant properties. By exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, Passiflora incarnata lessens tension, alleviates pain, reduces inflammation, and regulates hormones, making it a valuable natural remedy for various nervous system disorders.

As a result, the plant's unique combination of antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties enables it to heal tissues, remove mucus, and open airways, effectively alleviating respiratory issues.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Passiflora incarnata reduce urine frequency by its sedative and anti-spasmodic properties, which calm the nervous system and relax the muscles in the urinary tract, allowing the bladder to hold more urine. Passiflora incarnata treat kidney stones by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the kidneys and contribute to the formation of kidney stones. properties_list.description: Passiflora incarnata contains various flavonoids and phenolic acids that act as antioxidants, which can help protect cells from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation.

The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Passiflora incarnata have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions associated with chronic inflammation.

Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used as an anxiolytic agent, and studies have shown that it can reduce anxiety in both animal and human subjects, possibly due to its sedative and anxiolytic effects.

The plant has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, which may be due to the presence of flavonoids and other secondary metabolites.
Passiflora incarnata has been reported to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain, possibly due to its ability to modulate the pain pathway and reduce inflammation.
The flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata have been found to exhibit hypotensive effects, which can help lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension.

Passiflora incarnata has been traditionally used as a sedative agent, and studies have shown that it can induce sedation and reduce anxiety, possibly due to its ability to modulate the GABA receptor and other neurotransmitter systems.
The plant has been found to exhibit antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps, possibly due to its ability to relax smooth muscle and reduce muscle tone.

Passiflora incarnata has been reported to have anticonvulsant properties, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of seizures in individuals with epilepsy, possibly due to its ability to modulate the GABA receptor and other neurotransmitter systems.
The flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata have been found to exhibit antihypertensive effects, which can help lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension.

parts_description: The most used parts of Passiflora incarnata for medicinal purposes are leaves, fruits, flowers, and roots. The leaves contain flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin, which exhibit sedative and anxiolytic properties, as well as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The fruits are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, including passifloric acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and may be used to treat insomnia and anxiety. The flowers contain flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as flavonoid glycosides, which may have sedative and anxiolytic effects. The roots contain flavonoids and alkaloids, including passifloric acid and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and may be used to treat anxiety and insomnia.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly passiflorine and harmane, and exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative properties, which may help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and other sleep disorders, as well as provide relief from pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress and neurodegenerative diseases.

parts_list.name: flowers

parts_list.name: roots

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness in individuals with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
preparations.preparation_overview: Passiflora incarnata tincture is a herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, hyperactivity, restlessness, epilepsy, nausea, muscle spasm, and menstrual cramps. It is a very commonly used preparation, particularly in traditional medicine. One of its most noticeable effects is sedation, which can help individuals fall asleep and stay asleep. The tincture is typically made from the fruits, leaves, roots, and stems of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which are combined in a solvent, usually ethanol or glycerin, to extract their bioactive compounds. The process of making Passiflora incarnata tincture is moderately difficult, requiring careful measurement and preparation of the plant materials as well as the solvent.
preparation_recipe: Gather equal parts of fresh Passiflora incarnata fruits, leaves, roots, and stems for the tincture.
Dry the collected plant material in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying to preserve its potency.
Combine the dried plant material in a clean glass jar to create the herbal blend for the tincture.
Steep the herbal blend in a neutral solvent, such as 80-proof vodka or glycerin, for several weeks to extract its active compounds.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids, then store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, seizures, and restlessness in children with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.
preparations.preparation_overview: Passiflora incarnata capsule is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a variety of conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, chronic pain, menopausal symptoms, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, epilepsy, migraine, hypertension, gastroesophageal reflux disease and schizophrenia. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness, making it one of the most popular herbal remedies globally. One of the most common effects of Passiflora incarnata capsule is its sedative properties, which help individuals fall asleep faster and improve the quality of sleep. The capsule is made from the roots, leaves and fruits of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which are harvested and then processed into a concentrated form. The process of making this preparation is moderately difficult, requiring a combination of traditional knowledge and modern manufacturing techniques to ensure its potency and efficacy.
preparation_recipe: Harvest the roots, leaves, and fruits of the Passiflora incarnata plant and dry them thoroughly.
Grind the dried roots, leaves, and fruits into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered roots, leaves, and fruits together in a ratio that suits the desired herbal blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal powder and seal them tightly.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to relieve anxiety, insomnia, restlessness and promote relaxation and sleep.
preparations.preparation_overview: Passiflora incarnata salve is a herbal remedy used to treat anxiety, insomnia, stress, restlessness, rheumatism, pain, nausea, and digestive issues. It's a commonly used preparation in herbal medicine, often found in health food stores and online. One of its common effects is sedation, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The salve is typically made with the roots, leaves, and stems of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which are combined with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. The process of making the salve is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbalism and the ability to accurately identify and prepare the plant material.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh roots, leaves, and stems of Passiflora incarnata and dry them thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Combine the dried roots, leaves, and stems in a clean glass jar and infuse them in a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil for several weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.

Add a small amount of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the herbal salve into clean glass containers and let them cool and solidify completely before using or storing them.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness in children, as well as to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes and premenstrual syndrome in women, and to reduce pain and anxiety associated with withdrawal from opioid addiction.
preparations.preparation_overview: Passiflora incarnata tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including insomnia, anxiety, hypertension, menstrual cramps, epilepsy, and restlessness. It is one of the most widely used herbal teas due to its effectiveness and ease of preparation. The primary effect of Passiflora incarnata tea is its sedative properties, which help individuals relax and fall asleep. This herbal tea is made from the leaves, roots, stems, and flowers of the Passiflora incarnata plant. To make Passiflora incarnata tea, leaves and other plant parts are typically steeped in hot water, and it is often consumed as a calming beverage.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves, roots, stems, and flowers of Passiflora incarnata from a trusted source.
Rinse the collected plant parts with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the leaves, roots, stems, and flowers in a large pot and cover them with cold water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the plant material, then let it cool before serving.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative effects.
preparations.preparation_overview: Passiflora incarnata suppository is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions such as insomnia, anxiety, migraine, depression, restless leg syndrome, menopause symptoms and menstrual cramps. This preparation is relatively uncommon in medical practice due to its limited availability and lack of widespread clinical trials. Its effects can vary significantly from person to person, which is also attributed to its uncommon use in conventional medicine. The suppository is made from the roots and leaves of the Passiflora incarnata plant. However, the process of creating the suppository is complex and requires significant expertise in herbal medicine, which contributes to its limited availability.
preparation_recipe: Dry the roots and leaves of Passiflora incarnata in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a suitable base material, such as cocoa butter or beeswax, to create a uniform suppository paste.
Mold the paste into suppository shape using a mold or by hand, and refrigerate to set.
Store the Passiflora incarnata suppositories in an airtight container in the refrigerator for later use.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Passiflora incarnata are experiencing dizziness due to its sedative properties, developing nausea as a result of gastrointestinal irritation, and experiencing vomiting if ingested in large quantities. Furthermore, individuals may show tremors and experience muscle weakness due to the plant's depressant effects on the central nervous system. In severe cases, developing respiratory depression can occur, especially when combined with other central nervous system depressants. Additionally, improperly using Passiflora incarnata may lead to experiencing sedation, showing altered mental status, and experiencing bradycardia, with the latter potentially causing decreased blood pressure, leading to hypotension.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Passiflora incarnata may interact with certain medications including sedatives, antidepressants, and blood thinners, requiring medical supervision.
Adhere to established dosages to minimize the risk of adverse effects and interactions.
Passiflora incarnata may lower blood sugar levels, potentially requiring dose adjustments or medication changes in individuals with diabetes.
Passiflora incarnata may cause uterine relaxation and inhibit labor, potentially leading to adverse outcomes.
Passiflora incarnata increases sedative effects of barbiturates, benzodiazepines, and other sedative medications.
Passiflora incarnata interacts with monoamine oxidase inhibitors, increasing serotonin levels and risk of serotonin syndrome.
Monitor liver function due to risk of liver damage from Passiflora incarnata extract.
Passiflora incarnata may cause sedation and respiratory depression in children.
Passionflower interacts with selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors, increasing serotonin levels which may lead to serotonin syndrome.
Monitor patients for dizziness, gastrointestinal disturbances, and changes in mental status.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Toxicology and applied pharmacology", the potential pharmacologic bases for herbal plant efficacy have been explored, but more research is needed to confirm the benefits and risks of these agents. However, the study does not provide specific information about the positive effects of Passiflora incarnata.

According to a study published by Annals of Epidemiology, Passiflora incarnata has been found to be effective in alleviating various health needs and disease conditions. The study suggests that this medicinal herb can be successfully administered in different forms to address a wide range of health problems. Additionally, its bioactive chemical ingredients have proven to be valuable as primary or supplemental therapies when carefully applied.

According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Passiflora incarnata is considered to have various therapeutic properties, including sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can aid in promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The plant is also reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it potentially useful in alleviating pain and inflammation. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata may have a positive impact on sleep quality, as it is believed to help regulate sleep patterns and duration.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Passiflora incarnata are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which helps in scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. This plant exhibits anxiolytic effects due to the presence of volatile oils, specifically geraniol and linalool, which interact with GABA receptors to produce a calming effect. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata possesses antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides and phenolic acids, that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. The analgesic, hypotensive, sedative, antispasmodic, anticonvulsant, and antihypertensive properties of Passiflora incarnata are also evident, and are believed to be mediated by its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Passiflora incarnata are primarily flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant also contains saponins like passifloric acid that exhibit sedative and anxiolytic effects. Alkaloids including harmine and harmaline contribute to its anxiolytic and antispasmodic activities. Glycosides like passiflorin and flavoglycosides possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, phenolic acids including chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid display antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Passiflora incarnata for medicinal purposes are tinctures, often used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to their sedative properties. Capsules are also available, providing a standardized dose of passiflorin and other active compounds for relief from stress and restlessness. Topical salves, typically applied to the skin, are used to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with injuries and skin conditions. The leaves and stems of Passiflora incarnata can be steeped to make a tea, which is consumed to promote relaxation and reduce symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Suppositories are occasionally used to treat insomnia and anxiety in individuals who have trouble swallowing or experience gastrointestinal issues, providing a localized delivery of the plant's active compounds.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Passiflora incarnata medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional due to the risk of adverse interactions with other medications, particularly sedative medications and certain antidepressants such as SSRIs and MAOIs, and to follow recommended dosages to minimize potential side effects and ensure efficacy. Additionally, it is crucial to monitor blood sugar levels, as Passiflora incarnata may affect blood sugar control. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should also avoid using this plant medicinally, as it may pose risks to the fetus or infant. Furthermore, individuals with liver issues should monitor their liver function, as Passiflora incarnata may cause liver damage. Finally, use Passiflora incarnata with caution in children and report any side effects to a healthcare professional.
